concerns.text: FR-006 mandates a memory limit of 7GB RAM, while User Story 2 (Acceptance Scenario 2) and SC-002 mandate a limit of 6GB RAM. These contradictory constraints create an unresolvable conflict for the implementation and testing phases.

SC-001 states 'The minimum delta in R² considered significant is ≥ 0.05... determined via a paired bootstrap test'. This conflates the *definition* of the success metric (the delta threshold) with the *methodology* for statistical validation. A success criterion should define the target value (e.g., 'VLM R² must exceed Baseline R² by ≥ 0.05'), while the statistical test (bootstrap) belongs in the methodology or testing plan, not as part of the success threshold definition itself.

Inconsistency in terminology: User Story 2 refers to the model as a 'lightweight, fine-tuned Vision-Language Model (VLM)' in the description, but Acceptance Scenario 2 specifies 'lightweight VLM (CLIP-based, <500M params)'. While likely referring to the same thing, the spec should consistently use the specific architecture name (CLIP-based) or the generic term (VLM) throughout to avoid ambiguity in implementation.

SC-005 references 'See US-3', but US-3 (Feature Importance) focuses on permutation importance and driver quantification. The validation of 'Model generalizability' via variance in R² scores across basins is more logically aligned with the evaluation phase of US-2 (Model Training/Evaluation) where the test set results are generated. The cross-reference may be misleading regarding the source of the data for this metric.
